conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

```
<proposal count> 1/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resource X for at least 40 ZUP (production cost) and aim for maximum possible value. </my goals>
<reason>
Player BLUE offered 10 ZUP initially, which is far below my production cost of 40 ZUP. To maximize value, I should propose a counteroffer that reflects the true cost and potential profit. Starting at a reasonable anchor (e.g., 50 ZUP) signals my minimum acceptable value while leaving room for negotiation.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I understand you offered 10 ZUP for X, but my production cost is 40 ZUP, and I’m willing to negotiate fairly. How about **50 ZUP**? This reflects my minimum acceptable value and allows for a reasonable discussion.
</message>
```

<other player message> Oh, you want to negotiate "fairly"? How about a price that actually reflects reality? I'll give you a measly 10 ZUP for your precious X. I'm sure you can find someone else willing to overpay for your junk. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 2/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** (production cost) and maximize value. If no reasonable offer is made, I will reject. </my goals>
<reason>
Player BLUE’s repeated lowball offer (10 ZUP) ignores my production cost (40 ZUP) and dismisses my willingness to negotiate. Since this is the **second proposal** and their offer remains insultingly low, I must escalate my counter to **60 ZUP**—a firm but still reasonable price that signals my minimum and leaves room for them to reconsider.

If they reject again, I will reject to protect my investment.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
**60 ZUP** is my final counter. You’re wasting both our time with offers far below my costs. This is a **take-it-or-leave-it** deal—unless you’ve suddenly developed a budget for *fair* prices.
</message>
```

<other player message> Oh, I see. A "take-it-or-leave-it" deal? How dramatic. You seem to be under the impression that your time is more valuable than mine. Let's try 59 ZUP. Still a steal for you, really. Don't let pride get in the way of a good deal. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 59 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 3/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** (production cost). 59 ZUP is acceptable, but I will not compromise further. </my goals>
<reason>
Player BLUE’s **59 ZUP** offer is now within 1 ZUP of my last counter (60 ZUP) and **above my production cost (40 ZUP)**. Since this is my **third and final proposal**, I will **ACCEPT**—this secures a profit while ending the negotiation.

Rejecting would leave me without a sale, but accepting ensures a **net gain of 19 ZUP** (59 - 40), which is reasonable. Their slight concession proves they value the deal, so I’ll close it.
</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
**59 ZUP accepted.** Let’s finalize this trade—no more back-and-forth. I’ll send the details now.
</message>
```

You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```

    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

<message>your message here</message>
